issues.title: `{N}` template placeholder leak in quiz factAnswer and distractor pool (pop-culture numeric facts)
issues.description: Literal curly-brace strings like `"{125}"`, `"{1938}"`, `"{1.7}"`, `"{1984}"` ship as the canonical `factAnswer` AND 3 of 4 quiz choices for pop-culture numeric facts. Confirmed by FOUR independent testers in this batch: quiz-quality (C-001), fun-engagement (lowlight #2), study-temple (HIGH #1 — root cause: `StudyQuizOverlay.svelte` does not call `displayAnswer()` to strip bracket markers), full-run (C-001 confirmed 4th tester on `pc_3_instagram_facebook_acquisition`). Confirmed affected facts: `pc_0_mash_finale_viewers` (choices `["{193}", "125", "{1954}", "{59}"]`), `pc_2_superman_debut_year` (choices `["{2015}", "1938", "{3}", "{2008}"]`), `pc_2_spawn_sales_record`, `pc_3_instagram_facebook_acquisition`, Transformers 1984 study question. Hit rate across sampled facts ≈33%, suggesting the entire `pc_*` numeric-answer subcategory is broken. Bug lives in TWO layers: (1) upstream deck-build template-substitution pipeline leaves `{N}` in the source `factAnswer`, (2) `StudyQuizOverlay.svelte` does not wrap answer labels in `displayAnswer()` the way CardCombatOverlay/CardExpanded/ShopRoomOverlay/ChallengeQuizOverlay do. Steam-review fuel — a 30-second reviewer who screencaps `["{2015}", "1938", "{3}", "{2008}"]` wins the review thread.

bestEvidence.metric: 33% hit rate on sampled pc_* numeric-answer facts; 4 testers converged
bestEvidence.bugFile: src/ui/components/StudyQuizOverlay.svelte (missing displayAnswer), plus upstream deck-build pipeline

Distractors are `"1977 Trinity"`, `"Lithium cobalt oxide (LiCoO₂)"`, `"Username from host machine name"`. None are person names. Cross-checked across two combat encounters — the broken distractor set is PERSISTENT, not random. Card is winnable by elimination without any actual knowledge of the answer, which trivializes FSRS scheduling (the system thinks the player 'knew it' when they pattern-matched). Root cause: distractor-pool builder samples from a global string pool without an answer-type filter. Likely affects other 'person-name answer' facts not sampled.

issues.title: Combat softlock after chargePlayCard throws `currentEncounterSeenFacts is not iterable` on continued-run state

issues.title: crit_lens relic uses Math.random() — co-op peers roll independent crits for same card play
issues.description: In relicEffectResolver.ts line 1647, the crit_lens relic's 25% crit chance uses a bare Math.random() call. In co-op mode, both clients independently compute damage for the shared enemy via delta submission. If one client crits and the other doesn't, the two deltas submitted to the host barrier will differ for the same card play. The host merges deltas additively, so the final reconciled enemy HP will be correct, BUT the crit outcome feedback shown to each player will differ — one sees a crit flash, the other doesn't. If the delta computation itself uses crit to modify the damage value that goes into the delta, the two clients will submit different damage values for the same card, leading to inflated or deflated total damage in the merged snapshot.

bestEvidence.coopMechanism: EnemyTurnDelta.damageDealt = preTurnSnapshot.currentHP - enemy.currentHP after all card plays; if crit doubles a card's damage on one client but not the other, the deltas diverge

reproductionSteps: Two co-op peers, both holding crit_lens relic
Either peer plays a Charge card during their turn
Peer A crits (25% chance), Peer B does not
Both submit EnemyTurnDelta to the barrier
Peer A's delta shows higher damageDealt than Peer B's for the same card
Host merges: result is sum of both deltas — enemy takes non-crit damage twice instead of crit + non-crit

issues.title: obsidian_dice relic uses Math.random() — co-op peers independently roll +50%/-25% multiplier
issues.description: In relicEffectResolver.ts line 1712, the obsidian_dice relic applies a 50/50 multiplier roll (+50% or -25%) using Math.random(). Same issue as crit_lens: in co-op, both clients resolve the effect independently against their local enemy copy. If one rolls high and the other rolls low, the damage deltas submitted to the turn-end barrier will disagree for the same card play event.

bestEvidence.coopMechanism: Same delta-submission path as crit_lens issue

reproductionSteps: Two co-op peers, at least one holding obsidian_dice relic
One client rolls the +50% branch, other rolls the -25% branch
Deltas diverge — merged result is the arithmetic sum of the two divergent outcomes

issues.title: Chess Tactics: interactive board never renders — fenPosition data mismatch causes silent fallback to text MCQ
issues.description:
The chess_tactics deck has quizResponseMode: 'chess_move', fenPosition, and solutionMoves on every fact. StudyQuizOverlay.svelte DOES import ChessBoard.svelte (line 7) and HAS the chess rendering branch (line 211: 'quizResponseMode === chess_move && chessContext'). The pipeline correctly forwards all chess fields through nonCombatQuizSelector → generateStudyQuestions → QuizQuestion. The board never renders because getPlayerContext() in chessGrader.ts throws when applying solutionMoves[0] to the stored fenPosition.

Root cause: The fenPosition stored in chess_tactics.json facts is the puzzle position AFTER the opponent's setup move (the position the player sees). getPlayerContext() expects the BASE FEN before the setup move and applies solutionMoves[0] to produce the puzzle FEN. Example: fact chess_tac_4172G has fenPosition='1rb3k1/pp1pB1p1/3Pp2p/q1p2n2/2P3Q1/2bB1P2/P1R4P/5KNR b - - 3 20' with solutionMoves=['e4g4','f5e3','f1e2','e3g4']. There is no piece at e4 in this FEN, so applying e4g4 throws. The catch block at StudyQuizOverlay.svelte line 77-79 silently sets chessContext=null. Board branch at line 211 is skipped. Falls back to text MCQ with no error visible to player or in console.

Contrast: chessPuzzleService.ts stores puzzle.fen as the BASE FEN correctly (before setup move). chess_tactics.json was authored with the wrong FEN convention. Also: raw puzzle ID tag '[#Goifm]' is leaked in question text — separate content fix needed.
reproductionSteps: Observe: 4 multiple-choice buttons with chess notation text (no chess board rendered)
Question shows 'White to move. [#Goifm]' — raw puzzle ID visible in brackets
Note: StudyQuizOverlay.svelte line 7 imports ChessBoard, line 211 has chess branch — the UI code IS present

issues.title: RL reward signal missing comboCount bonus since TurnState field was removed — all trained models are undertrained on charge-correct behavior
issues.description: The `calculateCombatReward` function in gym-server.ts awards `0.1 * ts.comboCount` for each correct charge. Since `comboCount` was undefined throughout all training runs (the field was absent from TurnState), JavaScript evaluated this as `0.1 * undefined = NaN`, which JSON-serializes as `null`. The Python env received `null` reward and raised TypeError on every correct charge, causing the training process to either crash or fall back to a default. This means ALL trained models (v2_500k, v2_2M, v2_masked_2M, v3_economy_2M) were trained with corrupted reward signals for the most important positive feedback loop in the game — rewarding correct charges and chain building. The models' learned strategies are therefore unreliable with respect to charge-vs-quick decisions and chain building behavior.

bestEvidence.metric: gym-server.ts:628 ts.comboCount was undefined throughout all training; reward for correct charges was NaN (null) for all 2M-step training runs

reproductionSteps: Inspect tests/playtest/rl/analyze.py reward signal: calculateCombatReward at line 628

issues.title: Foresight (0 AP cost) is strictly dominant — always played, never suboptimal

The 0 AP cost makes it impossible to make a wrong decision about Foresight — it is always correct to play. This is the definition of a dominant strategy card: no opportunity cost, positive return. In state-03-19 (zero damage hand), Foresight's free draw effectively replaced a dead card slot with two live draws at 0 AP cost. In state-03-14, Foresight was played optimally before a killing blow. In state-03-04, it was identified as the setup before any kill sequence. A card with 0 AP cost and a positive draw effect has no meaningful downside and should not exist as a deck staple without a trade-off.

issues.title: Reckless attack card becomes zero-damage dead card against any block-stacking enemy
issues.description: In state-03-09 (Tenure Guardian with 12-18 block) and state-03-16 (Proctor with 6+ block, no Piercing available), Reckless(QP) dealing 6 base damage is entirely absorbed by enemy block. issues.title: The Curriculum Phase 2 QP-immunity reduces player effective actions to 1 per turn
issues.description: The Curriculum's Phase 2 (_quickPlayDamageMultiplierOverride = 0.0) makes every Quick Play attack deal 0 damage. Combined with the Charge AP surcharge (+1 AP), a player with standard 3 AP can execute at most 1 Charged attack per turn. In state-03-12, 3 of 5 hand cards (two Strikes and Heavy Strike) were completely dead as QP attacks, and the viable Charge sequence consumed all 3 AP. This reduces the player's agency to a single action per turn — a dramatic reduction from the normal 2-3 actions. While mechanically interesting as a design concept, the implementation creates a 'no good play' state not because of interesting strategic tradeoffs but because the mechanic hard-limits the player's action economy. The fight feels like waiting rather than decision-making.

issues.title: Floors 7-16 too safe: avg 1-4 dmg taken allows HP recovery, removing mid-game tension
issues.description:
Floor-by-floor analysis shows that after a moderate floor 6 encounter (9 avg dmg, 18.3 turns), floors 7-16 become very safe (1-4 avg dmg taken, 0% death rate). This creates a bimodal difficulty curve: challenging at floors 1-6, then a long plateau from 7-16, then a wall at floor 18.

Consequence: experienced players enter floor 18 at avg 105 HP (above starting 100 HP) — they have fully healed during the mid-game. The wall at floor 18 is experienced from near-full health, making it feel unfair rather than climactic.

issues.title: Quick Play vs Charge decision degenerated: 82% charge rate shows experienced players near-always charge
issues.description:
From 200 experienced-profile sim runs: 82% of all card plays are charge plays, 18% quick plays. Avg damage per charge: 9.2. Avg damage per quick play: 3.0 — a 3.1x ratio.

The Quick vs Charge decision was designed as the core player expression mechanism ('do you know this fact?'). In practice, high-accuracy players (76-80% charge accuracy) find charging almost always dominant because:
1. Charge Correct multiplier (1.5x base) + chain multipliers (up to 3.5x) + mastery bonuses = far exceed quick play output
2. AP surcharge (CHARGE_AP_SURCHARGE = 1) means charging costs 1 extra AP — but with 3 AP, a player can charge twice and quick-play once, making the choice mathematical not meaningful
3. The only time Quick Play is correct is when the player genuinely doesn't know the answer — but the game doesn't distinguish between 'strategic quick play' and 'don't know quick play'

For a new player, this might not be an issue (forced to quick play more). For a 10+ hour veteran, the decision has collapsed.

bestEvidence.metric: 200 experienced runs: totalQuickPlays=16614 (18%), totalChargedPlays=75757 (82%), dmg/QP=3.0, dmg/CC=9.2, ratio=3.1x

reproductionSteps: Aggregate totalQuickPlays vs totalChargedPlays across all runs

issues.title: Docker warm container crashes on __rrScenario.load() for combat scenarios — ascension visual testing blind
issues.description: Docker warm-container visual testing is unreliable for combat scenarios. issues.title: Attempt A (2-peer Docker lockstep) not feasible — BroadcastChannel transport cannot cross container boundaries
issues.description: The multiplayer transport implementation (multiplayerTransport.ts) uses BroadcastChannel for peer-to-peer messaging. BroadcastChannel is a browser same-origin API — messages only reach tabs within the same browser context. Two separate Docker containers each have isolated Chromium instances. No cross-container BroadcastChannel is possible without a shared WebSocket server or similar bridge. Therefore, a true 2-peer headless lockstep test cannot be conducted with the current infrastructure. The test was downgraded to Attempt B (single-peer seed determinism via Vitest).

bestEvidence.mechanism: BroadcastChannel API — tab-scoped, not cross-process or cross-container

reproductionSteps: Attempt to have both join the same BroadcastChannel
Messages will not be received across containers
issues.resolvedBy: BroadcastChannel transport limitation documented, not a game bug

issues.title: Docker SwiftShader WebGL drawCalls always -1 (not observable in this environment)
issues.description: The phaserPerf.drawCalls field returns -1 in Docker SwiftShader. The GL draw call count (renderer.gl?.drawCount) is not available when SwiftShader is running. This means draw call budgeting cannot be validated in Docker. Real GPU testing required for draw call verification.
issues.resolvedBy: SwiftShader limitation, not a game bug

issues.title: PASS: 50 rapid quickPlayCard+endTurn iterations produce zero errors or race conditions
issues.description: Rapid input mashing (50 play+endTurn cycles with no delay) produced zero game errors, zero race conditions, and no UI errors. All 50 plays and 50 turn ends executed successfully. The game handles rapid automated input gracefully.

issues.title: Viewport resize via JS dispatch does not change --layout-scale (expected: Playwright viewport is fixed)
issues.description: window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize')) with no actual viewport size change does not alter --layout-scale (stayed at 1.5). This is expected behavior - the CSS variable is driven by the actual viewport dimensions, not artificial resize events. In Playwright, window.resizeTo() is sandboxed and ignored. Actual resize testing requires Playwright viewport parameter change or multiple viewport configurations. The canvas getBoundingClientRect returned 42x28px which is mid-animation transform (not a bug - per testing.md Layout dump is not ground truth). Screenshot at 1920x1080 shows correct layout with all elements in frame.
issues.resolvedBy: Playwright viewport limitation, not a game bug

issues.title: PASS: Shop renders correctly at 500g with 3 items; no zero-gold scenario available via __rrPlay
issues.description: Shop scenario renders correctly: shows 500g gold display, 3 items (Whetstone 150g, Iron Shield 150g, Vitality Ring 150g). The __rrPlay API does not expose a setGold() method, so forcing 0-gold state requires a different approach (spawn with gold:0 parameter). Shop did not crash. forceHand() is also not in __rrPlay API - empty hand state cannot be force-tested via current API. These are API coverage gaps, not game bugs.

issues.title: PASS: Game handles corrupt localStorage save gracefully - loads hub without crash
issues.description: Corrupting 4 of 5 localStorage save keys (rr_save, rr_analytics_queue, terra-review-prompt-state, card:currentScreen) with invalid JSON did not crash the game. Hub scene loaded normally after corruption. Combat scenario also loaded normally. This confirms robust save data error handling - the game falls back to defaults when save data is corrupt.

issues.title: PASS: Memory stable across 3 consecutive combats (350-353MB, <1% growth)
issues.description: Memory usage across 3 consecutive combat scenario loads: combat1_start=350.3MB, combat1_end=351.0MB, combat2_start=350.2MB, combat2_end=352.6MB, combat3_start=353.2MB, combat3_end=352.6MB. Maximum growth: 2.9MB (0.83%) across 3 combats - well below the 10% threshold for leak suspect. gameObjectCount=36 and textureCount=109 were stable across all 3 combats, confirming proper cleanup on scenario reload.

issues.title: PASS: Zero game logic errors in __rrLog and recentErrors across combat/shop/reward/hub
issues.description: Across combat-basic, shop, reward-room, and hub-fresh scenarios, window.__rrLog.filter(e=>e.type==='error') returned 0 entries and window.__rrDebug().recentErrors returned [] in all cases. The 4 console errors collected were all ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED for static asset fetches (Docker environment artifact) and 1 Web Worker blob URL warning. Zero game logic errors.
